description: The markitdown library is designed as a stream-oriented conversion pipeline that transforms various file formats into Markdown. At its center, the Core Orchestrator manages a registry of specialized converters, using file-type detection to route input streams to the appropriate handler. The architecture is highly extensible, supporting standard web formats, complex office documents with mathematical preservation, and AI-native media transcription. It further extends its reach via an OCR layer for vision-based extraction and an MCP server interface that exposes these capabilities to AI agents.

components.name: Core Orchestrator
components.description: The central management hub of the library. It handles converter registration, file type detection (guessing), and the primary dispatch logic for conversion requests.

components.name: Input Normalization & Dispatching
components.description: Acts as the primary entry point for the library, responsible for resolving various input types (local paths, URLs, binary streams) into a standardized, seekable binary stream and initial metadata. It handles URI parsing and extracts hints from sources like HTTP headers.

components.name: Format Detection & Metadata
components.description: Provides the intelligence layer for identifying the content type and encoding of the input stream. It combines fast heuristics (file extensions, MIME types) with deep inspection (via Magika) to produce a prioritized list of format "guesses" used to select the appropriate converter.

components.name: Converter Orchestration Engine
components.description: Manages the registry of available converters (both built-in and external plugins) and implements the core execution logic. It iterates through registered converters and format guesses, managing stream state and error aggregation until a successful conversion is achieved.

components_relations.relation: Provides normalized streams and user-provided hints to generate prioritized format guesses.
Triggers the internal conversion loop once the input is prepared and metadata is extracted.
Consumes StreamInfo objects to match the current stream against the accepts criteria of registered converters.
calls

components.name: Standard & Web Converters
components.description: Handles common text-based and web formats. It utilizes specialized Markdownify logic to transform HTML, RSS, and Wikipedia content into clean Markdown.

components.name: Web & Syndication Adapters
components.description: Handles the extraction of content from dynamic and structured web sources, including HTML pages, Wikipedia articles, and RSS/Atom feeds. It performs initial DOM cleaning using BeautifulSoup and metadata extraction (e.g., titles, feed entries) before passing content to the transformation engine.

components.name: Markdown Transformation Engine
components.description: The core logic for converting HTML DOM structures into clean, safe Markdown. It extends the standard markdownify library with custom rules for link sanitization, image data-URI truncation, and ATX-style heading enforcement to ensure the output is optimized for AI data preparation.

components.name: Standard Format Converters & Common Model
components.description: Manages the conversion of standard text-based formats like CSV and Jupyter Notebooks into Markdown tables and code blocks. It also defines the DocumentConverterResult schema, which serves as the unified output contract for every converter in the subsystem, ensuring consistent downstream processing.

components_relations.relation: Delegates the conversion of extracted HTML snippets and article bodies into standardized Markdown.
Encapsulates extracted content and metadata into the DocumentConverterResult model for return to the orchestrator.
Utilizes the engine to clean and format HTML content found within Jupyter Notebook cells or other text-based structures.
calls
calls

components.name: Office & Document Converters
components.description: Manages complex binary formats including PDF, DOCX, and PPTX. It includes a specialized recursive descent parser for converting Office Math (OMML) to LaTeX to preserve mathematical expressions.

components.name: Office Format Converters
components.description: Implements the Strategy pattern for Microsoft Office and Email formats. It leverages specialized libraries (e.g., mammoth, python-pptx) to extract text and structure while maintaining document integrity.

components.name: Office Math Engine
components.description: A specialized utility component that performs recursive descent parsing of Office Math Markup Language (OMML). It translates XML-based math nodes into LaTeX strings to ensure mathematical formulas are accurately represented in Markdown.

components.name: PDF Conversion Engine
components.description: Manages the complex task of extracting content from layout-oriented PDF files. It includes custom logic for merging fragmented text lines and a coordinate-based reconstruction engine for building Markdown tables.

components.name: Azure Document Intelligence Adapter
components.description: Provides a cloud-based conversion path using Azure AI Document Intelligence. This component acts as an adapter for the external service, handling document analysis requests and mapping the structured service response back to Markdown.

components_relations.relation: Invokes pre_process_docx utility to delegate transformation of embedded mathematical XML nodes to the oMath2Latex engine.
calls
calls

components.name: Media & AI Converters
components.description: An AI-native component that handles non-textual media. It uses LLMs and transcription services to convert audio and images into descriptive Markdown text.

components.name: Audio Processing Engine
components.description: Manages the conversion of audio files (MP3, WAV, etc.) into Markdown. It extracts technical metadata like sample rate and artist info before delegating to a transcription pipeline that normalizes audio formats and performs speech-to-text.

components.name: Visual Media Analysis
components.description: Interprets static images by combining physical metadata (dimensions, GPS) with AI-generated descriptions. It leverages multimodal LLMs to "see" the image and produce a descriptive Markdown representation.

components.name: OCR-Driven Document Conversion
components.description: An advanced extension layer that handles documents with non-selectable text (scanned PDFs, images of spreadsheets). It treats document pages as visual inputs and uses vision-capable LLMs to extract text and layout information.

components_relations.relation: utilizes the same multimodal LLM prompting and image encoding patterns established in the Visual Media Analysis component to interpret individual document pages as visual scenes.
share a common architectural pattern of combining "Technical Metadata Extraction" with "AI-Native Content Generation," though they target different media modalities.
calls
calls

components.name: Extensibility & OCR Layer
components.description: Provides the plugin architecture for the library, including OCR-enhanced converters that use vision-based LLMs to extract text from images and complex document layouts.

components.name: OCR-Enhanced Conversion Service
components.description: Provides vision-based text extraction and specialized converters for complex document formats. It uses LLMs to interpret images and layouts from PDFs, Excel sheets, and Word documents, converting them into structured Markdown.

components.name: Plugin Extension Framework & Samples
components.description: Defines the standard interface and registration mechanism for external library extensions. It demonstrates how to add support for new file formats (like RTF) as standalone packages that integrate seamlessly with the core orchestrator.

components_relations.relation: Uses the registration pattern defined by the framework to expose OCR-enabled converters to the main library.
Provides the architectural template (via register_converters and converter interfaces) that the OCR service implements to provide its specialized functionality.
Dispatches text/HTML streams for Markdown transformation.
Dispatches binary document streams (PDF/DOCX) for structural parsing.
Dispatches audio/image streams for AI-based transcription and description.
Invokes plugin registration and dispatches streams to OCR-enhanced handlers.
Registers new converters into the central registry during initialization.
